originalTitle: Barney Frank: One of the first openly gay US congressmen dies aged 86
summary: After a decades-long career in Congress, he spent his final weeks in hospice care at his home in Maine.
content:
Former US congressman Barney Frank, who famously took on Wall Street and was one of the first known openly gay representatives, died on Tuesday night, US media reports. He was 86.

Frank, a Democrat who represented southern Massachusetts in the House of Representatives for over three decades, had been in hospice care at his home in Maine since April.

He will be remembered as a trailblaser for LGBT rights, as the first member of Congress in a same-sex marriage, and for helping to overhaul financial regulations after the 2008 financial crisis.

"He was, above all else, a wonderful brother. I was lucky to be his sister," Frank's sister Doris Breay told NBC Boston.

"He notified everybody that he was in hospice, so it was just a matter of time. He was certainly at peace with himself," Jim Segel, Frank's former campaign manager, told Axios.

"He certainly left a mark, and he was a leader on civil rights, on gay rights, on leading other marginalised communities, and then he helped the country get through the 2008 financial crisis, which was the most significant recession, depression, almost since 1930," Segel said.

Frank served in Congress from 1981 to 2013.

He was a major architect of the Dodd-Frank Act, which created new regulatory bodies and tightened restrictions on banks in the wake of the 2008 Great Recession.

The Dodd-Frank Act, named for Frank and fellow Democrat Sen Chris Dodd of Connecticut, was a historic overhaul of banking regulations in response to the subprime mortgage crisis that helped trigger the recession.

In 2010, then-President Barack Obama signed the legislation into law. In 2018, the Trump administration preserved part of the law while also loosening some restrictions - the "biggest rollback of bank rules" that decade, Time Magazine said at the time.

Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi paid tribute to Frank on X, calling him "an exceptional legislator, whose name is synonymous with the strongest consumer financial protections in history and whose advocacy helped forge a fairer future for all of our children".

On Capitol Hill, Frank was a vocal supporter of ending the "don't ask, don't tell policy" that kept gay and lesbian US military servicemembers from serving openly.

He also fought for legislation which would have banned workplace discrimination against LGBT workers.

"Prejudice is based on ignorance," Frank told The Boston Globe in 2011, as he prepared for retirement.

"And the best way to counterbalance it is with a living example, with reality,"

While in hospice, Frank did a number of interviews with US media, commenting on his life's work, the current political mood and the travails of the left.

"I'm filled with disgust at the current state, but optimism that it's going to get better," he told CNN's Jake Tapper earlier this month.
chineseTitle: 美国首批公开同性恋身份国会议员之一 Barney Frank 去世，享年86岁
chineseSummary: 在国会度过了数十年的职业生涯后，他在缅因州的家中度过了最后几周的临终关怀。
chineseContent:
据美国媒体报道，前美国国会议员巴尼·弗兰克（Barney Frank）周二晚去世，他以挑战华尔街而闻名，也是最早公开同性恋身份的代表之一。他86岁了。

弗兰克是一名民主党人，三十多年来一直在众议院代表马萨诸塞州南部，自四月份以来，他一直在缅因州的家中接受临终关怀。

他将作为 LGBT 权利的开拓者、第一位同性婚姻的国会议员以及在 2008 年金融危机后帮助改革金融监管而被人们铭记。

“最重要的是，他是一个很棒的兄弟。我很幸运能成为他的妹妹，”弗兰克的妹妹多丽丝·布雷伊告诉美国全国广播公司波士顿频道。

“他通知所有人他正在接受临终关怀，所以这只是时间问题。他当然对自己很平静，”弗兰克的前竞选经理吉姆·西格尔告诉 Axios。

“他确实留下了印记，他是民权、同性恋权利和其他边缘化社区的领导者，然后他帮助国家度过了 2008 年金融危机，那是几乎自 1930 年以来最严重的衰退和萧条，”西格尔说。

弗兰克于 1981 年至 2013 年在国会任职。

他是《多德弗兰克法案》的主要制定者，该法案在 2008 年经济大衰退后创建了新的监管机构并收紧了对银行的限制。

《多德-弗兰克法案》以弗兰克和康涅狄格州民主党参议员克里斯·多德的名字命名，是对银行业监管的一次历史性改革，以应对引发经济衰退的次贷危机。

2010年，时任总统巴拉克·奥巴马签署该法案成为法律。 2018年，特朗普政府保留了部分法律，同时放松了一些限制——《时代》杂志当时称，这是十年来“银行规则的最大倒退”。

前众议院议长南希·佩洛西在 X 上向弗兰克致敬，称他是“一位杰出的立法者，他的名字是历史上最强大的消费者金融保护的代名词，他的倡导有助于为我们所有的孩子打造一个更公平的未来”。

在国会山，弗兰克大力支持结束“不问不说政策”，该政策阻止美国同性恋军人公开服役。

他还争取立法禁止工作场所歧视 LGBT 工人。

2011 年，弗兰克在为退休做准备时告诉《波士顿环球报》：“偏见源于无知。”

“平衡它的最好方法是用活生生的例子和现实，”

在临终关怀中心期间，弗兰克接受了多次美国媒体采访，评论了他一生的工作、当前的政治情绪和左派的阵痛。

“我对目前的状况充满厌恶，但对情况会变得更好感到乐观，”他本月早些时候告诉美国有线电视新闻网 (CNN) 的杰克·塔珀 (Jake Tapper)。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
